topics.title: Types, val & var
topics.description: Learn Kotlin's basic types and the difference between val (immutable) and var (mutable) bindings.

lessons.title: val vs var
lessons.body: val is an immutable binding — it can't be reassigned after initialisation. var is mutable. Prefer val.
lessons.code:
val pi = 3.14159
var score = 0
score = 10  // OK
// pi = 3   // Error: val cannot be reassigned

lessons.title: Basic types
lessons.body: Kotlin has Int, Long, Double, Float, Boolean, Char, and String. All types are objects — there are no primitives from the developer's perspective.
lessons.code:
val age: Int = 30
val name = "Alice"      // inferred String
val active = true       // inferred Boolean

lessons.title: String templates
lessons.body: Embed expressions directly in strings with ${...} or just $variableName.
lessons.code: val msg = "Hello, $name! You are ${age + 1} next year."

lessons.title: Type inference
lessons.body: Kotlin infers types when you initialise a variable. Explicit annotations are optional but recommended for public APIs.
lessons.code:
val count = 42        // Int
val ratio = 0.5       // Double
val flag = false      // Boolean

topics.title: Null Safety
topics.description: Use Kotlin's type system to eliminate NullPointerExceptions at compile time.

lessons.title: Nullable types
lessons.body: By default, variables cannot be null. Add ? to the type to allow null.

lessons.title: Safe call operator ?.
lessons.body: ?. calls a method or accesses a property only if the receiver is not null. Returns null otherwise.

lessons.title: Elvis operator ?:
lessons.body: ?: provides a default value when the left-hand side is null.

lessons.title: let for null checks
lessons.body: ?.let { } runs the lambda only when the value is non-null, passing the non-null value as it.

topics.title: Data Classes & Sealed Classes
topics.description: Model your domain with Kotlin's data classes (value objects) and sealed classes (restricted hierarchies).

lessons.title: data class
lessons.body: A data class automatically generates equals(), hashCode(), toString(), and copy(). Perfect for DTOs and value objects.
lessons.code:
data class User(val id: Int, val name: String, val email: String)

val alice = User(1, "Alice", "alice@example.com")
val clone = alice.copy(email = "new@example.com")

lessons.title: Destructuring declarations
lessons.body: Data classes support destructuring — unpack properties into local variables.

lessons.title: sealed class
lessons.body: A sealed class restricts its subclasses to the same package. This enables exhaustive when expressions without an else branch.
lessons.code:
sealed class Result<out T>
data class Success<T>(val data: T) : Result<T>()
data class Error(val message: String) : Result<Nothing>()

lessons.title: Exhaustive when
lessons.body: When used as an expression with a sealed class, the compiler enforces handling every subclass.

topics.title: Coroutines & Flow
topics.description: Write asynchronous, non-blocking code using Kotlin coroutines and reactive streams with Flow.

lessons.title: suspend functions
lessons.body: A suspend function can be paused and resumed without blocking a thread. It can only be called from another suspend function or a coroutine.

lessons.title: Coroutine builders
lessons.body: launch starts a fire-and-forget coroutine. async returns a Deferred — await() to get the result.

lessons.title: Dispatchers
lessons.body: Dispatchers.IO for I/O work. Dispatchers.Default for CPU-intensive tasks. Dispatchers.Main for UI updates on Android.

lessons.title: Flow for streams
lessons.body: Flow is a cold asynchronous stream. Emit values with emit(); collect them with collect {}.
